title: ISic002761. Fragment of a magical text
body:
Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/FF745AII
1[---]sign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedΦΕ[---]
2[---]ΜΕΘΕΞ ἔξελθ(ε) διάβολ̣ε
3sign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpreted Ἀδωνα<ι> sign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpreted
4sign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpreted Ε[.]Ι Σα-
5βαὼθ ΑΡ̣ΑΚΙΑ
6sign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpreted[---]

l.2 subscript

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag; edition source: https://www.zotero.org/groups/382445/items/FF745AII

2[---]ΜΕΘΕΞΕΞΕΛΘΔΙΑΒΟ.Ε
3sign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedΑΔΩΝΑsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpreted
4sign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedsign, uninterpretedΕ[·]ΙΣΑ

l.2 subscript

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of Pugliese Carratelli

translation

commentary

The text appears to include a number of magical names, as well as magical characters. Line 5 may be 'Abraxia'.

bibliography

  • 18.0415 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000796
  • Pugliese Carratelli 1953 183-184 fig.2 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001651
Line 1 not recorded Interlineation line 1 to 2
